political.s: Southern Company PAC donations favor Republicans significantly, with 15% rep ratio (15,000 of 48,500) despite claiming left-leaning donations of $181K. The company engages in substantial lobbying ($11.2M in 2024) focused on regulatory and energy policy matters.

charity.s: Southern Company invested over $85 million in grants and sponsorships in 2024, with focus on education, health, environment, and community development. However, analysis indicates some charitable giving may be strategically deployed to influence regulatory relationships and political positions.
charity.focus: Education, health and human services, civic/community projects, environment, arts/culture, racial equity initiatives

environment.s: Company achieved 49% GHG reduction from 2007 baseline by 2024 and targets net-zero by 2050. However, audits reveal questionable renewable energy accounting practices—claiming 100% ownership credit for jointly-owned projects and potentially double-counting renewable energy credits, inflating climate progress claims.

labor.s: Southern Company is recognized as a leading employer with strong DEI programs (81% DEI training participation in 2022). However, CEO compensation vastly exceeds worker pay at 416:1 ratio, well above S&P 500 median of 324:1, raising equity concerns despite workplace recognition awards.

dei.s: Southern Company maintains pro-DEI stance with $225M commitment to racial equity through 2025, 81% employee DEI training, and Top 50 DiversityInc recognition for 8 consecutive years. However, recent SEC filings show company omitted DEI disclosures from business sections, signaling strategic retreat amid regulatory pressure.

animals.s: Not applicable—Southern Company is an energy utility with no animal testing activities or products.

guns.s: Not applicable—Southern Company is an energy utility with no firearms manufacturing or sales operations.

privacy.s: Southern Company operates telecommunications subsidiary (Southern Linc) handling customer data and manages extensive customer information for utility services. Privacy practices not extensively documented in public disclosures reviewed.

execPay.s: CEO Chris Womack earned $23.89M in 2024 with 416:1 CEO-to-worker pay ratio, significantly above S&P 500 median. Executive compensation structure heavily weighted toward stock/bonuses (93.8%) rather than salary, creating incentive misalignment with worker welfare and customer interests.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
